# Bundled third-party Python packages

`bundled-site-packages.zip` contains the pure-Python source trees of all
third-party packages shipped with the engine. The bundler ships a single
zip (not a directory tree) because `ios_framework` flattens data files
into the framework root, and multiple package `__init__.py` files would
collide there. The single-zip layout mirrors how `python3.14.zip` is
delivered.

At first launch `EGPluginRuntime` extracts the zip to
`Library/Caches/EGPythonBundledSitePackages/` and appends that directory
to `sys.path`.

## Contents

| Package | Version | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `requests`            | 2.34.x | depends on the items below |
| `urllib3`             | 2.7.x  | required by `requests` |
| `certifi`             | 2026.x | trust store (`cacert.pem`) |
| `charset_normalizer`  | 3.4.x  | required by `requests`; mypyc native speedups stripped — pure-Python `md.py` fallback is used |
| `idna`                | 3.16.x | required by `requests` |
| `cachetools`          | 7.1.x  | LRU/TTL cache primitives |

## Updating

```sh
cd exteraGram/EGPluginEngine/Python
TMP=$(mktemp -d)
pip3 install --target="$TMP" requests cachetools
find "$TMP" \( -name "*.so" -o -name "*.pyd" -o -name "*.dylib" \) -delete
find "$TMP" -name "__pycache__" -type d -exec rm -rf {} +
rm -rf "$TMP/bin"
(cd "$TMP" && zip -qr - .) > site-packages/bundled-site-packages.zip
rm -rf "$TMP"
```

`*.so` files are platform-specific and have no value on iOS — pure-Python
fallbacks are always shipped alongside them by upstream.

## sys.path order

The extracted bundled directory is appended to `sys.path` *after* the
user-writable site-packages at `Documents/EGPlugins/site-packages`, so
plugins that drop a newer version of e.g. `requests` into the writable
location will override the bundled copy.
